items.title: OpenAI shipped the GPT-5.6 family
items.oneliner: OpenAI launched Sol, Terra, and Luna, pushing frontier performance into a clearer price-performance ladder for production teams.

items.title: ChatGPT became a desktop work agent
items.oneliner: ChatGPT Work now browses, edits files, and uses apps, turning the assistant into a real operating surface.

items.title: Meta opened Muse Spark 1.1
items.oneliner: Meta gave Muse Spark 1.1 a 1 million-token window and agent tooling, raising pressure on API incumbents.

items.title: AI is generating brain-targeted videos
items.oneliner: EPFL is generating videos to activate specific brain regions, making personalization a neuroscience problem, not just a product one.

items.title: The EU targeted Meta's addictive design
items.oneliner: The EU says Instagram and Facebook breach the DSA, signaling algorithmic engagement design is now a regulatory liability.

items.title: AI music scraping is getting harder to ignore
items.oneliner: AI-generated Spotify artists are allegedly lifting from real musicians, turning copyright risk into a platform integrity problem.

items.title: AI data center buildout hit local resistance
items.oneliner: More than $130 billion in US AI data center projects were blocked or delayed, so compute scarcity is political now.

items.title: A 744B MoE runs on a slow PC
items.oneliner: Colibri runs GLM-5.2, a 744B MoE, on roughly 25 GB RAM by streaming experts from disk.

items.title: A kid-safe AI tutor needs sub-second latency
items.oneliner: Ello says teaching 5-year-olds needs responses under 1000 ms, exposing how most agent stacks still feel too slow.

items.title: Rust 1.97.0 tightened the toolchain
items.oneliner: Rust 1.97.0 changed default symbol mangling and improved Cargo diagnostics, which matters when debugging large polyglot systems.
